title: Azalea keysii
intro: The Azalea keysii, also known as the Keys' Azalea, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the rhododendron family, characterized by its vibrant pink or white blooms. Traditional herbal medicine has attributed various health benefits to Azalea keysii, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Azalea keysii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Azalea keysii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as to promote wound healing and immune system function.
taxonomy: Azalea keysii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. More specifically, it is part of the genus Rhododendron and is considered to be a member of the section Azalea. Within the Ericaceae family, Azalea keysii is closely related to other species of Rhododendron. Its classification places it within the order Ericales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. This classification highlights Azalea keysii's unique characteristics and its position within the plant kingdom.
